title: A Farewell by Coventry Patmore — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
With all my will, but much against my heart,
We two now part.
My Very Dear,
Our solace is, the sad road lies so clear.
It needs no art,
With faint, averted feet
And many a tear,
In our opposèd paths to persevere.
meaning: "A Farewell" by Coventry Patmore is a sad and love poem consisting of 25 lines. The poet explores themes of sad and love through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "With all my will, but much against my heart, We two now part....", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Coventry Patmore's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
